examples.text: قُلْ هَاتُوا بُرْهَانَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
examples.english: Say, ‘Produce your evidence, if you are telling the truth.’

senses.gloss: to ratify, to certify, to substantiate
a male given name, Sadiq, Sadeq, or Sadegh

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of صَدَقَ (ṣadaqa, “to be sincere, to be right, to come true”). Compare Hebrew צדיק (tsadiq, “honest, righteous”).

Form III from the root ص د ق (ṣ d q).

From صَادِق (ṣādiq), active participle of صَدَقَ (ṣadaqa).
